+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubevirt-13311-specify-the-service-accounts-mounted-into-the-vm",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubevirt: Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM",
+    "description": "Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM. Requested by 6+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kubevirt deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your virtctl version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt\nhelm list -n kubevirt 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kubevirt installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubevirt configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt\nkubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt\n```\nI want to mount an EKS service account containing an IAM role."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM",
+        "description": "Thanks alot for the detailed description\nTrying to make sense of your solution, so i will rephrase what i understand and please correct me if i am wrong. First of all..\n\n> It would check to see if a service account is mounted as volume.\n\nHow might this look ? I envision something like this (in the\n```yaml\nEnvironment:\n    # SOME AWS ENV VARIABLES HERE\nMounts:\n    /var/run/secrets/eks.amazonaws.com/serviceaccount from aws-iam-token (ro)\n    /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from kube-api-access-j8cqt (ro)\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt\nkubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Specify the service accounts mounted into the VM\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Thanks alot for the detailed description\nTrying to make sense of your solution, so i will rephrase what i understand and please correct me if i am wrong. First of all..\n\n> It would check to see if a service account is mounted as volume.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Environment:\n    # SOME AWS ENV VARIABLES HERE\nMounts:\n    /var/run/secrets/eks.amazonaws.com/serviceaccount from aws-iam-token (ro)\n    /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from kube-api-access-j8cqt (ro)",
+        "/var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from kube-api-access-j8cqt (ro)",
+        "apiVersion: v1\nitems:\n- apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1\n  kind: VirtualMachine\n  metadata:\n    annotations:\n      kubevirt.io/latest-observed-api-version: v1\n      kubevirt.io/storage-observed-api-version: v1\n    creationTimestamp: \"2024-11-20T18:18:47Z\"\n    finalizers:\n    - kubevirt.io/virtualMachineControllerFinalize\n    generation: 2\n    name: ubuntu\n    namespace: default\n  spec:\n    running: true\n    template:\n      metadata:\n        creationTimestamp: null\n        name: ubuntu\n      spec:\n        architecture: amd64\n        domain:\n          cpu:\n            cores: 8\n            dedicatedCpuPlacement: true\n          devices:\n            filesystems:\n            - name: service-account\n              virtiofs: {}\n          firmware: {}\n          machine:\n            type: q35\n          mem"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
